all.en: Daniel had wanted to climb Yushan for almost ten years, ever since he was in college.
all.zh: 丹尼爾自大學時代起，就想攀登玉山將近十年了。

all.en: Last winter he finally won a permit in the park's official online lottery.
all.zh: 去年冬天他終於在國家公園的官方線上抽籤中抽中入山許可。

all.en: He spent three months training with weekend hikes around Yangmingshan.
all.zh: 他花了三個月在陽明山周圍進行週末健行訓練。

all.en: His backpack contained warm layers, headlamps, energy bars, and a small first-aid kit.
all.zh: 他的背包裡裝著保暖衣物、頭燈、能量棒與一個小型急救包。

all.en: On the night before the climb, he met his guide Kuo at the visitor center.
all.zh: 登山前夜，他在遊客中心遇到了他的嚮導郭先生。

all.en: Kuo was a quiet man in his fifties who had summited Yushan over sixty times.
all.zh: 郭先生是位五十多歲的沉默男子，曾登頂玉山超過六十次。

all.en: He warned Daniel that the wind could be harsh and the altitude could cause nausea.
all.zh: 他提醒丹尼爾風勢可能很強，高度也可能讓人感到噁心。

all.en: They started hiking at dawn through a forest of red cypress and cool morning mist.
all.zh: 他們在黎明時分啟程，穿越紅檜森林與清涼的晨霧。

all.en: Birds called above their heads as sunlight broke through the high canopy.
all.zh: 鳥鳴在頭頂響起，陽光穿透高聳的樹冠灑落而下。

all.en: After several hours, the trail grew steeper and Daniel began to feel his legs burn.
all.zh: 幾個小時後，步道變得陡峭，丹尼爾的雙腿開始發燙。

all.en: Kuo set a slow, steady rhythm and told him to drink water every twenty minutes.
all.zh: 郭先生維持緩慢而穩定的節奏，並提醒他每二十分鐘要喝水。

all.en: They reached Paiyun Lodge in the late afternoon and rested under heavy gray clouds.
all.zh: 他們在午後抵達排雲山莊，在厚重的灰雲下休息。

all.en: Other climbers shared instant noodles, hot chocolate, and stories of past expeditions.
all.zh: 其他登山客分享著泡麵、熱可可，以及過去登山的故事。

all.en: Daniel tried to sleep but the thin air made his breathing feel shallow and rushed.
all.zh: 丹尼爾試著入睡，但稀薄的空氣讓他呼吸顯得急促又短淺。

all.en: At two in the morning, the lodge bell rang for the final push to the summit.
all.zh: 凌晨兩點，山莊的鐘聲響起，宣告攻頂的最後衝刺即將開始。

all.en: Headlamps formed a glowing chain that snaked slowly up the dark mountain ridge.
all.zh: 頭燈組成一條發光的鎖鏈，緩緩蜿蜒爬上漆黑的山稜。

all.en: The wind grew louder and ice crystals stung Daniel's cheeks with every step.
all.zh: 風聲愈來愈響，每一步冰晶都刺痛著丹尼爾的雙頰。

all.en: Halfway up, he wanted to turn back, but Kuo gently encouraged him to continue.
all.zh: 半途中，他想放棄折返，但郭先生溫和地鼓勵他繼續前進。

all.en: He focused only on the small circle of light from his lamp and his own breathing.
all.zh: 他只專注於頭燈映出的那一小圈光，以及自己的呼吸。

all.en: Finally, just before sunrise, they crawled up the last metal chain to the peak.
all.zh: 終於，就在日出前，他們爬上最後一段金屬鐵鍊抵達山頂。

all.en: The summit marker stood quietly against a sky that was slowly turning pink.
all.zh: 山頂標誌靜靜佇立，背後的天空正逐漸轉為粉紅色。

all.en: Below them, an ocean of clouds stretched endlessly across the central mountain range.
all.zh: 在他們腳下，雲海無邊無際地延伸至整個中央山脈。

all.en: The sun emerged like a slow drop of fire on the eastern horizon.
all.zh: 太陽如同一滴緩緩落下的火焰，從東方的地平線升起。

all.en: Daniel felt tears form in his eyes although he could not fully explain why.
all.zh: 丹尼爾感覺淚水在眼眶中打轉，雖然他無法完全解釋原因。

all.en: Kuo simply smiled and patted his shoulder, sharing the silent moment beside him.
all.zh: 郭先生只是微笑著拍拍他的肩膀，與他共享這靜默的時刻。

all.en: Other climbers cheered, hugged strangers, and held flags from their hometowns.
all.zh: 其他登山客歡呼著、擁抱陌生人，並舉起家鄉的旗幟。

all.en: After twenty minutes, the wind grew dangerous and Kuo signaled it was time to descend.
all.zh: 二十分鐘後，風勢轉強危險，郭先生示意該下山了。

all.en: Going down was harder on Daniel's knees but his heart felt unusually light.
all.zh: 下山對丹尼爾的膝蓋更為吃力，但他的心情格外輕盈。

all.en: They returned to Paiyun, packed quickly, and continued back through the forest.
all.zh: 他們回到排雲山莊，迅速整理行李，繼續穿越森林下山。

all.en: Along the way, they spotted a Mikado pheasant stepping carefully across the trail.
all.zh: 途中，他們瞥見一隻帝雉小心翼翼地穿越步道。

all.en: Daniel froze, watching the bird's blue feathers shimmer in the filtered light.
all.zh: 丹尼爾凝住不動，看著那隻鳥的藍色羽毛在透過樹葉的光線中閃爍。

all.en: By evening, his boots were muddy and his shoulders ached, but he was smiling.
all.zh: 傍晚時分，他的登山鞋沾滿泥土，肩膀痠痛，卻一直微笑著。

all.en: On the drive back to Chiayi, he thanked Kuo for refusing to let him give up.
all.zh: 回嘉義的車上，他感謝郭先生當時沒有讓他放棄。

all.en: Kuo replied that the mountain teaches patience to those who listen carefully.
all.zh: 郭先生回答說，山會教導那些願意用心聆聽的人何謂耐心。

all.en: Back home, Daniel framed a photo of the sunrise and hung it above his desk.
all.zh: 回到家後，丹尼爾將那張日出照片裱框，掛在書桌上方。

all.en: Every morning he glanced at it before starting his work and felt steadier inside.
all.zh: 每天早晨他在開始工作前都會看一眼那張照片，內心便覺得更加穩定。

all.en: He began writing short essays about each leg of the journey for an outdoor magazine.
all.zh: 他開始為一本戶外雜誌撰寫關於旅程中每個階段的短篇散文。

all.en: Readers wrote in asking for advice on permits, gear, and training schedules.
all.zh: 讀者紛紛來信詢問入山許可、裝備與訓練時程的建議。

all.en: Daniel replied to every letter, remembering how lost he had felt before his own first climb.
all.zh: 丹尼爾回覆了每一封來信，想起自己第一次登山前的迷惘。

all.en: A small online group formed around his articles and began planning a group expedition.
all.zh: 在他的文章周圍漸漸聚集起一個小小的線上社團，並開始籌畫一次團體登山。

all.en: Daniel invited Kuo to be their guide and Kuo agreed without a moment of hesitation.
all.zh: 丹尼爾邀請郭先生擔任他們的嚮導，郭先生毫不猶豫地答應了。

all.en: They scheduled the climb for the following autumn when skies tend to be clear.
all.zh: 他們把登山行程安排在隔年的秋天，那時的天空通常較為清朗。

all.en: Daniel volunteered to mentor two first-time hikers on their weekend training walks.
all.zh: 丹尼爾自願擔任兩位首次登山者的指導員，陪他們進行週末訓練徒步。

all.en: He shared everything he had learned about pacing, hydration, and managing fear quietly.
all.zh: 他分享了自己學到的所有關於配速、補水以及悄悄處理恐懼的方法。

all.en: Standing again at the base of Yushan, he realized the mountain had given him a new community.
all.zh: 再次站在玉山腳下時，他意識到這座山已賜予了他一個全新的社群。
